title: Decimal measurement fields get precision constraints by measurement type (uses match directive)
description: Decimal fields capturing physical measurements (height, weight, temperature, blood pressure, etc.) get an auto-generated precision constraint based on the measurement's typical clinical precision. DEFAULT PRECISION TABLE: Height fields (name contains HEIGHT or HT) → 2 decimals; Weight fields (name contains WEIGHT or WT) → 2 decimals; Temperature fields (name contains TEMP) → 1 decimal; Blood pressure fields (name contains BP, SBP, DBP) → 0 decimals (integer); Heart rate / pulse fields (name contains HR or PULSE) → 0 decimals (integer); Other decimal fields → 2 decimals default. AUTO-APPLIED CONSTRAINT: `. >0 and . =round(${field_name}, <decimals>)`. The >0 clause provides positive-value sanity; the round() clause enforces precision. AUTO-APPLIED MESSAGE: 'Value must be positive, with no more than <N> decimal place(s).' UNIVERSAL SCOPE per §0. Override: customer library specifies different precision (or none); carry forward as-authored.
rationale: Clinical measurements have well-established precision conventions: height-in-cm to 2 decimals, weight-in-kg to 2 decimals, body temperature to 1 decimal, blood pressure and pulse as integers. Allowing data-entry users to record height=170.123456 cm produces false-precision artifacts in the data that mislead downstream analyses. The round-and-equate constraint forces user input to match the expected precision, surfacing precision-mismatched entries at entry time rather than in monitoring. The >0 clause prevents the common typo of negative values for physical measurements which can't be negative.

flag.message: Decimal field ${field.name} on ${form.form_id} — apply precision constraint per measurement type. The skill's match dispatch sets field.constraint_decimals; emit `field.constraint = '. >0 and . =round(${${field.name}}, ${field.constraint_decimals})'` and `field.constraint_message = 'Value must be positive, with no more than ${field.constraint_decimals} decimal place(s).'` Customer-library overrides preserved.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §28 during Phase B.1d. This is the rule that ORIGINALLY MOTIVATED the match directive (B.1c-2) — flagged in B.0 audit inventory as F5 PRECISION_TABLE. Uses match on field.name with a 10-case dispatch (HEIGHT/HT, WEIGHT/WT, TEMP, BP/SBP/DBP, HR/PULSE) and a default case for other decimals. Structured detection (filter on field.type=decimal) and structured dispatch (match writes field.constraint_decimals per case). The actual constraint-expression construction (with field-name interpolation) is the remaining soft piece — same DSL gap noted for §22 (sibling-name interpolation in effect expressions). When string-interpolation lands as an effect feature, the constraint and constraint_message ensures promote to fully-structured.

Corrected kind from 'structured' to 'hybrid' during B.1d validation pass. The match directive (10-case dispatch on field.name → field.constraint_decimals) IS valid structured work. The soft directive described constructing the field.constraint string with field-name interpolation, which requires expression-string templating — a known DSL gap. Schema forbids match + soft coexisting. Resolution: keep match (the per-case decimal assignment lands properly), drop soft, document the templating gap in the flag's message. When string-interpolation lands as an effect feature, the constraint-expression construction can promote back to structured.
